title: [HandshakeToHW] The `JoinOp` lowering does not resolve all backedges
body:
When lowering the following handshake IR directly to HW

```mlir
handshake.func @main(%arg0: none, %arg1: none) -> (none) {
  %res = join %arg0, %arg1 : none, none
  return %res : none
}
```
one get's the following errors:

```
../test/Conversion/HandshakeToHW/test_join.mlir:4:10: error: backedge of type `'none'`still in use
  %res = join %arg0, %arg1 : none, none
         ^
../test/Conversion/HandshakeToHW/test_join.mlir:4:10: note: see current operation: %0 = "builtin.unreal
ized_conversion_cast"() : () -> none
../test/Conversion/HandshakeToHW/test_join.mlir:4:10: note: used by %chanOutput, %ready = esi.wrap.vr <
<UNKNOWN SSA VALUE>>, <<UNKNOWN SSA VALUE>> : none
../test/Conversion/HandshakeToHW/test_join.mlir:4:10: error: backedge of type `'i1'`still in use
  %res = join %arg0, %arg1 : none, none
         ^
../test/Conversion/HandshakeToHW/test_join.mlir:4:10: note: see current operation: %0 = "builtin.unreal
ized_conversion_cast"() : () -> i1
../test/Conversion/HandshakeToHW/test_join.mlir:4:10: note: used by %chanOutput, %ready = esi.wrap.vr <
<UNKNOWN SSA VALUE>>, <<UNKNOWN SSA VALUE>> : none
../test/Conversion/HandshakeToHW/test_join.mlir:4:10: remark: abandoned 2 backedges
  %res = join %arg0, %arg1 : none, none
         ^
module {
  hw.module @main(%arg0: !esi.channel<none>, %arg1: !esi.channel<none>, %clock: i1, %reset: i1) -> (out
Ctrl: !esi.channel<none>) {
    %handshake_join0.out0 = hw.instance "handshake_join0" @handshake_join_2ins_1outs_ctrl(in0: %arg0: !
esi.channel<none>, in1: %arg1: !esi.channel<none>) -> (out0: !esi.channel<none>)
    hw.output %handshake_join0.out0 : !esi.channel<none>
  }
  hw.module @handshake_join_2ins_1outs_ctrl(%in0: !esi.channel<none>, %in1: !esi.channel<none>) -> (out
0: !esi.channel<none>) {
    %rawOutput, %valid = esi.unwrap.vr %in0, %3 : none
    %rawOutput_0, %valid_1 = esi.unwrap.vr %in1, %3 : none
    %0 = builtin.unrealized_conversion_cast to none
    %1 = builtin.unrealized_conversion_cast to i1
    %chanOutput, %ready = esi.wrap.vr %0, %1 : none
    %2 = comb.and %valid, %valid_1 : i1
    %3 = comb.and %ready, %2 : i1
    hw.output %chanOutput : !esi.channel<none>
  }
}
```

As far as I can tell, this is caused by neither connecting the `outValid` not the `outData` signals.

Fixing the `outValid` is simple, e.g., by connecting it to the "and" of all input valids. On the other hand, a solution for the data is not that obvious. Connecting it to any of the input's data will not work, as no input might be a `NoneType`. Furthermore, there are no `NoneType` constants in HW.

Ideally, `NoneType` inputs would not have any kind of data fields, but that would complicate some other things.

CC @mortbopet
